| Flag | Argument | Default | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--kanata-loop-start`  | `<pc>` | -  | Start PC of the loop body. Reported in the summary; not used to gate iterations. Hex (`0x...`) or decimal accepted. |
| `--kanata-loop-end`    | `<pc>` | -  | End PC of the loop body. Each retire of this PC counts as one iteration. |
| `--kanata-loop-skip`   | `<N>`  | 0  | Warmup iterations to ignore before the window opens. `0` records from the first iteration. |
| `--kanata-loop-record` | `<X>`  | 1  | Iterations to capture after warmup. Must be >= 1. Simulator exits when this many iterations have retired. |

## How iterations are counted

An iteration is counted only when the instruction at `--kanata-loop-end` __retires__. Mispredicted or flushed fetches of that PC do not advance the counter, so the recorded window is always exactly `record` complete iterations of the loop body.
input.new_string:
| Flag | Argument | Default | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--kanata-loop-start`  | `<pc>` | -  | First PC of the loop body. The window opens on the first **fetch** of this PC after warmup, so all pre-loop code (CRT, init, library calls) is filtered out. Hex (`0x...`) or decimal accepted. |
| `--kanata-loop-end`    | `<pc>` | -  | Back-branch PC of the loop body. Each retire of this PC counts as one iteration. |
| `--kanata-loop-skip`   | `<N>`  | 0  | Warmup iterations to discard before the window opens. `0` opens the window the first time we enter the loop body. |
| `--kanata-loop-record` | `<X>`  | 1  | Iterations to capture after warmup. Must be >= 1. Simulator exits cleanly when this many iterations have retired. |

## How the window opens and closes

- Each retire of `--kanata-loop-end` advances the iteration counter, whether the gate is open or closed. Mispredicted/flushed fetches of that PC do not advance it.
- The gate **opens** the first time `--kanata-loop-start` is fetched *after* `skip_iters` end-PC retires have been observed. From that fetch onward, all I/L/R/S records are emitted, and the on-disk Konata cycle counter is realigned to the simulator's absolute cycle so timing inside the window is correct.
- The gate **closes** when the iteration counter reaches `skip_iters + record_iters`. The simulator prints the summary line and returns `0` from its run loop (no `std::exit`, so waveform / pc-trace cleanup still runs).

The recorded window is always exactly `record_iters` complete iterations of the loop body, and the trace contains *only* code reachable from the loop's start PC.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Kanata Loop-Window Pipeline Dump

Restrict a Kanata or Clarity pipeline dump to a chosen number of iterations of one specific loop body, after a configurable warmup. The simulator exits as soon as the window closes and reports per-window IPC.

## When to use it

- Inspect steady-state pipeline behavior of a hot loop in Konata.
- Get IPC for the loop body alone, ignoring warmup and tail effects.
- Produce a small, shareable pipeline trace instead of a multi-GB full-run log.

## Flags

All flags are optional and only take effect with `--kanata` or `--clarity`. `--kanata-loop-start` and `--kanata-loop-end` must be specified together.

| Flag | Argument | Default | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--kanata-loop-start`  | `<pc>` | -  | Start PC of the loop body. Reported in the summary; not used to gate iterations. Hex (`0x...`) or decimal accepted. |
| `--kanata-loop-end`    | `<pc>` | -  | End PC of the loop body. Each retire of this PC counts as one iteration. |
| `--kanata-loop-skip`   | `<N>`  | 0  | Warmup iterations to ignore before the window opens. `0` records from the first iteration. |
| `--kanata-loop-record` | `<X>`  | 1  | Iterations to capture after warmup. Must be >= 1. Simulator exits when this many iterations have retired. |

## How iterations are counted

An iteration is counted only when the instruction at `--kanata-loop-end` __retires__. Mispredicted or flushed fetches of that PC do not advance the counter, so the recorded window is always exactly `record` complete iterations of the loop body.

## Picking the start/end PCs

Use `objdump -d` on your ELF and pick:

- `start` = first instruction of the loop body (typically the branch target / loop label).
- `end`   = last instruction of the loop body (typically the back-branch, or the instruction immediately before it if you only want taken iterations).

Both PCs must be reachable in the simulated execution. If `end` is never retired, the window never opens and the simulator runs to completion normally.

## Output

While the gate is closed (warmup), no `I` / `C` / `R` records are emitted, but the internal cycle counter still advances so timing inside the window is accurate. When the window closes, the logger writes to spdlog:

```
Kanata loop window [pc 0x...start..0x...end]: <iters> iters, <insns> insns, <cycles> cycles, IPC=<ipc>
```

and the simulator exits with status 0. The dump file contains a self-consistent Kanata trace for those iterations only and can be opened directly in Konata.

## Example

```
vsim --elf bench.elf \
     --kanata bench.kanata \
     --kanata-loop-start 0x80001234 \
     --kanata-loop-end   0x80001260 \
     --kanata-loop-skip  100 \
     --kanata-loop-record 8
```

Skips the first 100 iterations, records the next 8, prints the IPC summary, and exits. `bench.kanata` contains those 8 iterations only.

## Constraints

- `--kanata-loop-*` requires `--kanata` or `--clarity`; otherwise startup fails.
- `--kanata-loop-start` and `--kanata-loop-end` must both be present or both absent.
- `--kanata-loop-record` must be >= 1.
- `--kanata` and `--clarity` remain mutually exclusive.
